figures.caption: Figure 4: Eye gaze preceding sentence onset predicts temporal context as effectively as eye gaze during speech. AUC (OvR) is reported, and baseline performance (0.5) is shown as a dashed line.
Table 4: Linear Mixed-Effects Model for Mean Vertical Gaze as a function of temporal context (pre-war baseline) and sentence timing (𝑁 = 245, 370 observations)

Figure 5: Model prediction results by prior context. Performance drops when temporal context changes, but remains above chance. ’-1s offset’ refers to the eye gaze window starting one second prior to sentence onset.
Figure 1: Valence, Arousal, and Dominance estimated from audio recordings and aligned with transcripts.
Figure 2: Gaze dynamics (GAMM) across temporal contexts around sentence onset.
Figure 3: Temporal dynamics of vertical gaze relative to sentence onset differ significantly for the pre-war context.
